meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve no design skills (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no design skills. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Analytics Setup to Solve No Design Skills
content.seo_description: Stop worrying about UI design. Learn how to leverage analytics to validate your app's value. A beginner-friendly checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: If you lack design skills, stop obsessing over pixels. Instead, implement behavioral analytics to track how users actually interact with your MVP. By measuring feature engagement, you validate your product's utility, turning your lack of design into a data-driven advantage that proves your app's value to future employers and users.
content.intro:
You stare at a blank white screen, paralyzed by the 'design gap.' You want to build, but you aren't a designer, and your buttons look like they were styled in 1998. The frustration is real, but here is the secret: users don't care about your padding; they care about whether your tool solves their problem.

By shifting your focus from aesthetic perfection to data-driven validation, you transform your app from a 'side project' into a professional case study. Using Antigravity to instrument your code and Claude Code to handle the implementation, you can gain deep insights into user behavior that no designer could provide. Let's build a product that works, not just one that looks pretty.

steps.title: Define User Success Events
steps.description: Identify the 3 core actions that represent a user getting value from your app (e.g., 'file_uploaded', 'analysis_completed').
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase and suggest 3 high-impact behavioral events that track user success. Format as a JSON schema.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review my core features and generate an event tracking schema for PostHog or Mixpanel.'

steps.title: Inject Analytics Middleware
steps.description: Create a centralized wrapper to capture these events without polluting your business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, reusable analytics middleware function in TypeScript that handles event triggering and property enrichment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a tracking service utility and integrate it into the main app entry point.'

steps.title: Instrument Key User Flows
steps.description: Add tracking to your UI components so you can see where users drop off.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Locate the primary user flow components and suggest where to inject the trackEvent calls for maximum visibility.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Inject trackEvent calls into the onboarding and submission components.'

steps.title: Implement Session Replay
steps.description: Use session recording to see exactly where users get confused by your interface.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Help me configure a lightweight session recording script that respects user privacy and doesn't impact bundle size.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Add PostHog session replay scripts to the head of the application.'

steps.title: Build the Validation Dashboard
steps.description: Create a view that aggregates your data to prove your app's value.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Construct a SQL query or dashboard configuration to visualize user retention and feature adoption based on the events we implemented.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Draft a dashboard configuration that highlights user growth and feature engagement metrics.'

pro_tips: Track 'frustration signals' like rapid clicking or repetitive input errors to identify UI pain points.
Always set your analytics to 'anonymized' mode to avoid GDPR headaches while you learn.
Use events to measure 'Time to First Value' (TTFV) instead of just page views.
Never track PII (Personally Identifiable Information) in your events; keep it strictly behavioral.
Monitor your event payload size to ensure your analytics implementation doesn't kill your page load speed.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, deploy your app to a free hosting provider like Vercel or Netlify, share the link in our community, and start tracking your first 10 real users.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting the 'design barrier' keep you from shipping your portfolio project. You have the tools to prove your value through data, even if you never touch a CSS file. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
